rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: ESP kernel module loaded (xfrm exploit vector)
rule.info: Goal: Detect loading of esp4/esp6 modules used in DirtyFrag xfrm exploit chain. These modules enable the IPsec ESP transforms that the exploit abuses for page-cache corruption.
False Positives: Legitimate IPsec/VPN hosts using strongSwan or Libreswan. Do NOT deploy on IPsec gateway hosts.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: AF_ALG socket from non-system binary (exploit primitive)
rule.info: Goal: AF_ALG crypto socket from binary outside /usr — key exploit primitive for DirtyFrag page-cache attack. Legitimate crypto tools (openssl, etc.) live in /usr/bin/.
False Positives: Custom crypto applications compiled to non-standard paths (rare).

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: splice/vmsplice from non-system binary (page-cache manipulation)
rule.info: Goal: Detect splice syscall from non-standard binary — core mechanism for DirtyFrag page-cache corruption. Standard system tools that use splice (cp, cat) live in /usr/bin/.
False Positives: Custom file-copy utilities using splice for zero-copy I/O.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: Executable file dropped in writable directory
rule.info: Goal: Detect staging of exploit binary (e.g. ./update) in temp or home dir. Microsoft observed ELF binary named './update' staged before exploitation.
False Positives: Legitimate software updates or build artifacts in temp dirs. Filter by excluding known update tools if noisy.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: Binary from temp directory running as root (post-exploitation indicator)
rule.info: Goal: Detect exploit binary running with root privileges from writable directory. This indicates successful exploitation or direct root access to staging area.
False Positives: Legitimate admin scripts in home directories run as root via cron or manual execution.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: Known exploit binary name pattern detected
rule.info: Goal: Match Microsoft-observed PoC naming (./update) and common LPE binary names from temp dirs. High confidence when combined with directory context.
False Positives: Legitimate binaries named 'update' — low risk given temp dir constraint.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: Known exploit binary executed via command line
rule.info: Goal: Detect PoC binary execution via auditd execve. Microsoft observed ./update as the PoC filename in live attacks. Matches with or without ./ prefix.
False Positives: Script named 'update' in current directory — low risk given exact argv[0] matching.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: Successful su to root (potential page-cache passwd corruption)
rule.info: Goal: Detect su → root which is the expected result of DirtyFrag /etc/passwd page-cache corruption. Qualys disclosed that the exploit empties the root password field in cached page, enabling su without password.
False Positives: Legitimate su to root by authorized admins. Cross-reference with 190800/190900 signals on same host for confirmation.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: su to root from unexpected parent (post-exploit escalation)
rule.info: Goal: Detect su to root from unusual parent process — DirtyFrag enables su without password prompt. Legitimate su typically comes from sudo/sshd/login/cron.
False Positives: Interactive admin using su from a custom shell or tmux/screen.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: /etc/passwd modified (DirtyFrag page-cache attack indicator)
rule.info: Goal: Detect changes to /etc/passwd — DirtyFrag corrupts root password field via page-cache. FIM may only trigger after cache flush or reboot since the exploit modifies in-memory page-cache, not disk directly (Qualys disclosure).
False Positives: Legitimate user account creation via useradd/usermod. Check syscheck.uname_after for who changed it.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: DirtyFrag mitigation config removed or tampered
rule.info: Goal: Detect attacker removing the modprobe blacklist mitigation for esp4/esp6/rxrpc. CloudLinux recommends creating /etc/modprobe.d/dirtyfrag.conf to block vulnerable modules.
False Positives: Admin reverting mitigation after kernel patch is applied (expected operation).

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: Post-exploitation recon as root from unusual parent
rule.info: Goal: Detect recon commands (cat /etc/shadow, find, whoami, id) as root from non-standard parent. Microsoft telemetry showed recon of system configuration after escalation.
False Positives: Admin running recon from custom scripts or automation tools.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: Post-exploitation config file edit as root
rule.info: Goal: Detect Microsoft-observed post-exploit pattern — editing GLPI LDAP auth config, PHP configs, and other application settings to establish persistence or modify authentication.
False Positives: Admin editing configs legitimately. Cross-reference with other DirtyFrag signals on same host.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: Mass PHP session file deletion (post-exploit anti-forensics)
rule.info: Goal: Detect deletion of PHP session files — Microsoft observed this as post-exploit action to hijack remaining sessions and destroy forensic evidence.
False Positives: Legitimate PHP session garbage collection (usually via cron with www-data user, not root). Check eventdata.user for root = suspicious.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43284: DirtyFrag exploitation chain confirmed (3+ signals in 300s)
rule.info: Goal: Fire when 3 or more atomic DirtyFrag signals occur from the same source within 5 minutes. This indicates active exploitation across multiple kill chain phases.
False Positives: Extremely unlikely — requires 3+ independent signals from staging, exploitation, and post-exploitation phases.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43500: RxRPC/rxkad module loaded (DirtyFrag RxRPC variant)
rule.info: Goal: Detect loading of rxrpc/rxkad modules — nearly never used on production servers. Core vector for CVE-2026-43500 page-cache attack via AF_RXRPC transport.
False Positives: AFS client systems using AF_RXRPC transport (extremely rare on production Linux servers). Safe to deploy on all non-AFS hosts.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43500: RxRPC module loaded by non-system binary (exploit staging)
rule.info: Goal: Increase confidence — rxrpc triggered by binary outside /usr = highly suspicious. No standard system utility loads rxrpc.
False Positives: None expected — rxrpc is not used by standard system utilities.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43500: su to root + RxRPC signals from same host (exploitation chain)
rule.info: Goal: Fire when 2+ CVE-43500 signals (RxRPC module + su/passwd) occur from the same source within 5 minutes. This confirms active exploitation — not just isolated module loading.
False Positives: Extremely unlikely — requires both RxRPC module loading AND su/passwd change from same host.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43500: /etc/passwd root entry modified (DirtyFrag page-cache corruption indicator)
rule.info: Goal: Detect modification specifically to the root line in /etc/passwd. Qualys PoC targets the first line to empty the password field. The syscheck.diff field shows what changed — matching 'root:' ensures we only fire on root entry modifications, not any user account change.
False Positives: Admin changing root shell or GECOS via usermod. Very low FP rate given root-line-specific matching.

rule.description: CVE-2026-43500: Repeated RxRPC module loading — exploitation in progress
rule.info: Goal: RxRPC module loaded multiple times from same source = active exploitation attempts. Module loading is normally a one-time kernel event.
False Positives: Virtually none — rxrpc module loading should never repeat in normal operation.
